microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's recovery **validation program** from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing deployment playbooks to sustain 45+ annual drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise fleet customers.

Led 0→1 development of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining self-service scheduling requirements that cut operational toil by 30% and transitioned execution to a scalable, repeatable model across partner teams.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive hardware validation capability, delivering a rack-level pilot program in 4 months with a **94% recovery rate**, surfacing critical defects and establishing a continuous performance monitoring framework.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract with rigorous failover protocol validation.

Scaled team leverage via AI-driven automation, building an internal AI agent that restructured planning workflows, cut **planning cycle time by 39%**, and increased drill throughput capacity by 21% without adding headcount.

Partnered with Finance and Engineering to build operational cost models using staffing plans and time-study data, informing TCO targets and unlocking **fleet-wide efficiency** gains across scaled deployment programs.
microsoft_2023: Championed adoption of AI-driven **automation workflows**, conducting demos and training sessions that drove utilization across 14 key teams and saved **37 engineering hours monthly** on manual operational touchpoints.

Shaped the product roadmap toward intent-based YAML generation by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, surfacing critical feature gaps and translating operational requirements into prioritized engineering deliverables.

Optimized data integrity and retrieval by implementing rigorous metadata standards and migrating documentation to an **AI-powered semantic search** tool, cutting lookup time by 83% and reducing manual labor touchpoints.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched regions 28% faster by securing cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework across 140+ teams to eliminate manual deployment bottlenecks.

Built a product strategy to reduce operational toil during region launches, running discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual effort** per region and roadmapping automation across critical deployment paths.

Engineered a **Power BI performance monitoring** dashboard to track operational toil and automation gaps across 140+ teams, enabling leadership to prioritize high-ROI investments and validate efficiency targets.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot transition by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping hardware dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and establishing robust failover protocols.

Facilitated Agile sprint ceremonies and backlog prioritization to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets**, stabilizing the developer intake process and accelerating the program toward scaled deployment milestones.

Drove cross-functional alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, reducing manual deployment touchpoints and accelerating the software release cycle by 25%.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end proposal operations and managing the scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals, reducing manual labor touchpoints via a new CRM deployment playbook.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and lifted conversions by 2.7% by executing a digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and validating performance through operational data and conversion metrics.

Improved leadership **TCO and pricing models** by conducting financial profitability analysis across 1,000+ project records, identifying and sealing margin leaks through data-driven cost and performance target recommendations.

tailoring_notes: TCO/ROI models: woven into microsoft_ft bullet#6 and pro_painters bullet#3 — mirrors JD's 'TCO and ROI models' preferred qualification directly.
Deployment playbooks: used in microsoft_ft bullet#1 and pro_painters bullet#1 — mirrors JD's 'deployment playbooks' requirement under 'You will' section.
Performance monitoring telemetry: surfaced in microsoft_ft bullet#3 and microsoft_2022 bullet#3 — maps to JD's 'performance monitoring telemetry for central teams and external partners'.
Failover protocols: embedded in amazon_robotics bullet#1 — directly mirrors JD language 'establishing robust failover protocols' under 'You will'.
Manual labor touchpoints / V2H ratio framing: used in amazon_robotics bullet#3, microsoft_2023 bullet#1, and pro_painters bullet#1 — maps to JD's 'eliminate manual labor touchpoints' requirement.

Pilot-to-scaled-deployment arc: amazon_robotics bullet#1 references 2,000-unit pilot transition; microsoft_ft bullet#1 references scaling from 2-person op — mirrors JD's 'concept through prototype, pilot, and scaled deployment' language.
